action.reasoning: The task requires producing a 4-chapter technical field guide with research, drafting, editing, a glossary, and assembly. This is too large for a single EXECUTE action. I will decompose it into parallel chapter subtasks (each handling research, draft, and edit for one chapter), a glossary subtask, and take the final assembly role myself. Each chapter subtask will produce its own chapter content. The glossary will be built in parallel. I will then assemble everything into the final guide.

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 1: Introduction to LLM Agent Memory Systems
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 1, which introduces the concept of memory in LLM agents, defines key terminology, and outlines the landscape of memory architectures. It must include original research, a full draft, and a consistency edit. The output will be used in the final assembly.

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 1 on introduction to LLM agent memory systems

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 2: Short-Term and Working Memory Mechanisms
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 2, covering short-term memory, context windows, attention mechanisms, and working memory patterns in LLM agents. It must include original research, a full draft, and a consistency edit. The output will be used in the final assembly.

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 2 on short-term and working memory mechanisms

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 3: Long-Term Memory Architectures
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 3, covering long-term memory systems including vector databases, knowledge graphs, episodic memory stores, and retrieval-augmented generation patterns. It must include original research, a full draft, and a consistency edit. The output will be used in the final assembly.

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 3 on long-term memory architectures

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 4: Advanced Memory Patterns and Future Directions
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 4, covering advanced topics such as memory consolidation, multi-agent memory sharing, memory debugging, and emerging research directions. It must include original research, a full draft, and a consistency edit. The output will be used in the final assembly.

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 4 on advanced memory patterns and future directions

subtasks.goal: Compile a unified glossary of terms used across all chapters
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces a comprehensive glossary of technical terms related to LLM agent memory systems. It should define key concepts consistently so that the final guide uses uniform terminology. The glossary will be integrated into the final assembly.

outputs.description: Unified glossary of terms for the LLM agent memory field guide

self_role.goal: Assemble the final field guide from all chapter drafts and the glossary, ensuring structural coherence and adding front matter

outputs.description: Final assembled 4-chapter technical field guide to LLM agent memory systems, including glossary and front matter

action.reasoning: Chapter 3 is a substantial technical writing task covering vector databases, knowledge graphs, episodic memory, and RAG patterns. It requires research, drafting, and editing. To manage scope and ensure quality, I will decompose into parallel research subtasks for each major topic, then have a dedicated drafting subtask and a final editing subtask that I will handle myself as the integrator. This ensures parallel discovery and a coherent final product.

subtasks.goal: Research vector database architectures for LLM long-term memory
subtasks.capsule: Investigate vector databases (e.g., FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus) used for storing and retrieving semantic embeddings in agent memory systems. Cover indexing strategies (HNSW, IVF), similarity metrics, scalability, and trade-offs. Output a structured research summary.

outputs.description: Research summary on vector database architectures for agent memory

subtasks.goal: Research knowledge graph approaches for agent memory
subtasks.capsule: Investigate knowledge graph-based memory systems for LLM agents, including graph construction from text, schema design, graph traversal for retrieval, and integration with LLM reasoning. Cover tools like Neo4j, NetworkX, and graph RAG patterns. Output a structured research summary.

outputs.description: Research summary on knowledge graph approaches for agent memory

subtasks.goal: Research episodic memory and RAG patterns for agents
subtasks.capsule: Investigate episodic memory stores (storing past interactions, experiences, and outcomes) and retrieval-augmented generation patterns that combine long-term memory with real-time generation. Cover memory consolidation, forgetting strategies, and hybrid retrieval. Output a structured research summary.

outputs.description: Research summary on episodic memory and RAG patterns for agents

subtasks.capsule: Synthesize the three research summaries into a cohesive, well-structured draft of Chapter 3. action.reasoning: Chapter 4 is a substantial technical writing task covering multiple advanced topics (consolidation, multi-agent sharing, debugging, future directions). To ensure depth and quality, I will decompose it into parallel research/drafting subtasks for each major topic, then integrate them with a consistency edit. This matches the pattern of other chapters and ensures thorough coverage within the call budget.

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on memory consolidation and distillation patterns
subtasks.capsule: Cover techniques for consolidating episodic memory into semantic knowledge, including summarization, clustering, and distillation methods. Discuss trade-offs between retention fidelity and storage efficiency. This forms the first major section of Chapter 4.

outputs.description: Draft section on memory consolidation and distillation patterns

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on multi-agent memory sharing and coordination
subtasks.capsule: Cover architectures for shared memory across multiple agents, including publish-subscribe patterns, memory marketplaces, conflict resolution, and privacy-preserving sharing. This forms the second major section of Chapter 4.

outputs.description: Draft section on multi-agent memory sharing and coordination

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on memory debugging, observability, and evaluation
subtasks.capsule: Cover techniques for debugging memory systems, including tracing, replay, memory audits, and quantitative evaluation metrics. Discuss tools and methodologies for diagnosing memory-related failures. This forms the third major section of Chapter 4.

outputs.description: Draft section on memory debugging, observability, and evaluation

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on emerging research directions and open problems
subtasks.capsule: Cover cutting-edge research directions such as neuro-symbolic memory, lifelong learning architectures, memory-augmented reasoning, and open problems in the field. This forms the fourth major section of Chapter 4.

outputs.description: Draft section on emerging research directions and open problems

self_role.goal: Integrate all section drafts into a cohesive Chapter 4, perform consistency editing, and produce the final chapter draft

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 4 on advanced memory patterns and future directions
